samples.text: A triangle has exactly three sides and exactly four sides at once. Is this logically consistent?
A triangle has precisely three and precisely four sides simultaneously. Does this make logical sense?
A three-sided figure has both three and four edges at the same time. Is this logically consistent?
Exactly three and exactly four are incompatible counts. A shape cannot have both. Is the described situation logically consistent?
A triangle has exactly three sides and exactly four sides at once. This topic was discussed last week. Is this logically consistent?
